title: pulicaria wightiana
intro: Pulicaria wightiana, also known as White Leaf or Wight's Pulicaria, is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family, native to the Western Ghats in India. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with its leaves and roots exhibiting anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities. The health benefits of Pulicaria wightiana include its ability to treat fever, sore throats, and skin conditions, as well as its potential to lower cholesterol levels and reduce blood sugar levels. The plant can be used in various herbal preparations, such as decoctions, infusions, and ointments, to harness its medicinal properties and provide relief from various ailments.
